title: The Turing Guide
summary: The Turing Guide, written by Jack Copeland, Jonathan Bowen, Mark Sprevak, Robin Wilson, and others and published in 2017, is a book about the work and life of the British mathematician, philosopher, and early computer scientist, Alan Turing (1912–1954).

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: The Turing Guide , written by Jack Copeland , Jonathan Bowen , Mark Sprevak, Robin Wilson , and others and published in 2017, is a book about the work and life of the British mathematician , philosopher , and early computer scientist , Alan Turing (1912–1954).

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: The book includes 42 contributed chapters by a variety of authors, including some contemporaries of Alan Turing. The book was published in January 2017 by Oxford University Press , in hardcover , paperback , and e-book formats.

sections.heading: Contents
sections.text:
The Turing Guide is divided into eight main parts, covering various aspects of Alan Turing's life and work:
The book includes a foreword by Andrew Hodges , a preface, notes on the contributors, endnotes, and an index.

sections.text:
The book has been reviewed by a number of journals, magazines, and professional organizations, including:
The book has also been featured online internationally, including in China .

sections.heading: Cover
sections.text: This artwork for the book's cover came about after a mock-up digital artwork in the style of the multiple images of Andy Warhol was produced. Jack Copeland then organized a more professional artwork, which became the basis for the eventual book cover. In 2023, the artwork was displayed as part of a digital art exhibition organized by the Computer Arts Society at the BCS in London .

sections.heading: Other languages
sections.text: A Chinese edition of the book was published in 2023 by Tsinghua University Press .
